title: Understand: food and energy in organisms
description: Check your understanding of food and energy in organisms in this set of free practice questions aligned to NGSS standards.
topic_path: Understand: food and energy in organisms

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describe what food provides to organisms?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: molecules that can be used for growth
energy that can power cell processes
new mitochondria that can carry out cellular respiration

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these statements about food are true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Food provides molecules that can be rearranged and incorporated into an organism’s tissues.
Food provides an energy source for an organism.
Food directly provides new mitochondria which an organism can use for cellular respiration.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describe what food provides to organisms?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Food provides molecules that can be used to grow and stay healthy.
Food provides energy that can power life processes.
Food provides new mitochondria that can carry out cellular respiration.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fats, sugars, and proteins are important food molecules. What do these types of molecules have in common?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: They all contain carbon atoms.
They all have the same molecular structure.
They are all built up into larger molecules during digestion.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fats, sugars, and proteins are important food molecules. Which statement about these types of molecules is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Fats, sugars, and proteins are broken down into smaller parts during digestion.
Fats and sugars contain carbon atoms, but proteins do not.
Fats, sugars, and proteins have the same molecular structure.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fats, sugars, and proteins are important food molecules. Which statement about these types of molecules is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Fats, sugars, and proteins are all carbon-based molecules.
Fats, sugars, and proteins have the same molecular structure.
Fats, sugars, and proteins are all built up into larger molecules during digestion.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these statements about cellular respiration is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The steps of cellular respiration occur in the cytosol and mitochondria.
Cellular respiration consists of a single chemical reaction that releases energy.
Organisms use cellular respiration to store food molecules in their cells.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these statements about cellular respiration is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: All of the steps of cellular respiration occur in the cytosol.
Cellular respiration is a series of chemical reactions that result in an overall release of energy.
Cells store food molecules through the process of cellular respiration.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these statements about cellular respiration is true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: All of the steps of cellular respiration occur in the mitochondria.
Cellular respiration consists of a single chemical reaction that stores energy.
Organisms use cellular respiration to get usable energy from food.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which table correctly shows the inputs and outputs of cellular respiration?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content:
Inputs | Outputs
- | -
oxygen | carbon dioxide 
glucose | water

Inputs | Outputs
- | -
carbon dioxide | water 
oxygen | glucose

Inputs | Outputs
- | -
carbon dioxide | oxygen 
water | glucose

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these are inputs to cellular respiration?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: carbon dioxide
water
oxygen
glucose

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these are outputs of cellular respiration?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: carbon dioxide
water
oxygen
glucose
